Title: Xylem systems genetics analysis reveals a key regulator of lignin biosynthesis in Populus deltoides

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: xylem secondary cell wall; transgenic roots
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Provides rigidity and mechanical strength to secondary cell walls and contributes to vascular water conduction; major determinant of biomass recalcitrance relevant to pulp and biofuel processing
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes lignin biosynthesis and increases lignin content
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key positive trans-acting regulator and likely master regulator of the lignin biosynthetic pathway during secondary cell wall formation
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: cis/local regulatory SNPs within MYB125 coding/UTR region; secondary cell wall formation context
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: xylem tissue in a diverse natural population; MYB125 overexpression in transgenic poplar hairy roots
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: lignin biosynthesis pathway transcriptional control during secondary cell wall formation
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: 4CL3; CSE; F5H; C3'H; PAL2; CCoAOMT1; PAL4; COMT1; C4H1/C4H2
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: 4CL3; CSE; F5H; C3'H; PAL2; CCoAOMT1; PAL4; COMT1; C4H1/C4H2
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates expression of lignin biosynthetic genes; overexpression significantly up-regulated 4CL3, CSE, F5H, C3'H, PAL2, CCoAOMT1, PAL4, and COMT1; local MYB125 SNPs showed trans-association with nine lignin-pathway genes
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed in xylem; positively correlated with lignin content; expression is much higher than close homolog MYB92 in the studied population
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in xylem during secondary cell wall formation
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MYB125 was identified by eQTL and coexpression network analysis as the only transcription factor among highly connected lignin-module hub genes with significant local-eQTLs. 2. MYB125 is directly connected to nine lignin biosynthetic genes in the xylem coexpression network, and local MYB125 SNPs were associated with expression of these genes and with lignin content. 3. MYB125 expression positively correlated with lignin content in the natural population. 4. Overexpression of MYB125 in transgenic poplar roots significantly up-regulated eight lignin pathway genes and increased acetyl bromide soluble lignin by 35% to 158%. 5. The study concludes that MYB125 acts as a positive transcriptional activator of lignin biosynthesis, likely in the branch where CSE and 4CL bypass the second HCT reaction.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Homologous to Arabidopsis MYB42/MYB85. A close paralog, MYB92 (Potri.001G118800), is expressed in xylem but at much lower level and with limited segregation; authors argue MYB125, not MYB92, explains the observed phenotype. In P. trichocarpa, MYB125 was previously shown to activate PAL4 and CCoAOMT1 promoters. One local SNP causes a Thr255Ala substitution, but causal nucleotide was not resolved.
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Actin2 promoter (constitutive, in transgenic hairy roots)
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate regulator for engineering wood composition and lignin content in poplar for bioenergy and pulp applications; may be useful as a master-regulator target, but increased lignin could elevate biomass recalcitrance depending on deployment goal
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, eQTL mapping, coexpression network analysis; GEO: GSE140232
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Increased lignin may increase biomass recalcitrance and processing cost for biofuel production; no growth penalty directly reported
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: local- and trans-eQTL analysis; coexpression network analysis; single-marker regression; transgenic overexpression in poplar hairy roots; RT-qPCR of target genes; acetyl bromide soluble lignin quantification
